subject: [PATCH v6 04/15] userfaultfd: test uffd VMA flags through the vma_flags_t API

2026-05-29.patch_summary: This patch updates the userfaultfd VMA flag helpers to use the vma_flags_t accessor, which is a more modern and unified API for accessing VMA flags. The change is made to accommodate config-gated per-mode masks, ensuring that the code remains compatible with different build configurations. The updated code uses the vma_test_any_mask() function to check for specific UFFD flags, replacing direct access to vm_flags. This change does not introduce any functional differences, as the same bits are being read and the masks fold to the same code as before.

2026-06-02.patch_summary: This patch updates the userfaultfd VMA flag helpers to use the vma_flags_t accessor, which is a single point of truth for available modes on the current build. The change ensures that the code remains compatible with config-gated per-mode masks and maintains identical behavior as before. The update affects several inline functions in include/linux/userfaultfd_k.h, replacing direct vm_flags checks with calls to vma_test_any_mask().

2026-06-03.patch_summary: This patch updates the userfaultfd VMA flag helpers to use the vma_flags_t accessor, which is a single point of truth for available modes on the current build. The change ensures that the code remains compatible with config-gated per-mode masks and maintains identical behavior as before. The update affects several inline functions in include/linux/userfaultfd_k.h, replacing direct vm_flags checks with calls to vma_test_any_mask().
